info.title: letmepost.dev API
info.description:
Open-source social media publishing API. Preflight validation, transparent errors, idempotency, stable platform versions.

The full developer documentation lives at [letmepost.dev/docs/](https://letmepost.dev/docs/). This reference is generated from the canonical Zod schemas at `packages/schemas/`.

**Auth:** Bearer key in the `Authorization` header. See [Authentication](https://letmepost.dev/docs/authentication/).
**Idempotency:** every write accepts `Idempotency-Key`. See [Idempotency](https://letmepost.dev/docs/idempotency/).
**Errors:** stable envelope with `code`, `rule`, `platformResponse`, `remediation`, `docUrl`, `ruleUrl`. See [Errors](https://letmepost.dev/docs/errors/).

tags.name: Posts
tags.description: Publish and read posts. Multi-target: a single `POST /v1/posts` fans out to up to 25 connected accounts.

tags.name: Accounts
tags.description: Connect, list, and disconnect platform accounts.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Mint and revoke API keys (dashboard session only).

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Register, test, and manage outbound webhook endpoints.

bearer.description: Mint an API key in the dashboard. See https://letmepost.dev/docs/authentication/.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Per-route request ceiling for this endpoint. A static contract — sliding-window remaining/reset live on the IETF-draft `RateLimit-*` headers (no prefix) when enforcement is active.

X-Request-Id.description: Per-request correlation id, also echoed inside the error envelope as `error.requestId`.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Recommended on every write. Replays within 24 hours return the original response; conflicting bodies surface as `409 idempotency_conflict`. See [Idempotency](https://letmepost.dev/docs/idempotency/).

Platform.description: Supported platforms. `tiktok` is currently in App Review — connect requests return `platform_not_enabled` until approval lands.

PostStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a single per-target post row. `canceled` is the terminal state reached via `DELETE /v1/posts/{id}` on a queued scheduled post.

ErrorCode.description: Canonical error codes. See https://letmepost.dev/docs/errors/ for per-code documentation.

ApiError.description: Stable error envelope. Every non-2xx response uses this shape. `docUrl` is always present; `ruleUrl` is present whenever `rule` is set.

message.description: Human-readable summary. Stable enough to log, not stable enough to switch on — switch on `code` and `rule`.

rule.description: The specific preflight rule or validator that failed (e.g. `bluesky.text.max_graphemes`, `targets.options.platform_mismatch`).

platformVersion.description: Pinned upstream API version the call targeted (e.g. `graph-v23.0` for Facebook).

platformResponse.description: Raw upstream platform response, when available.

remediation.description: Actionable next step for the caller — short, imperative.

docUrl.description: Link to the docs page for this `code`.

ruleUrl.description: Link to the docs page for this preflight `rule`. Present whenever `rule` is set.

requestId.description: Per-request correlation id, also echoed in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

traceId.description: OpenTelemetry trace id, when tracing is active.

error.message: Bluesky posts are capped at 300 graphemes; this body is 312.
error.remediation: Trim 12 graphemes, or split into a thread.

MediaInput.description: A single media item attached to a post. Exactly one of `mediaId` / `url` / `bytesBase64` must be present.

altText.description: Per-platform alt-text caps apply (e.g. Bluesky 2000, Twitter 1000).

mediaId.description: References a previous `POST /v1/media` upload. Preferred for production — bytes already live on our CDN.

url.description: Public URL we fetch from your CDN at publish time. Must respond 200 to anonymous GET.

bytesBase64.description: Inline base64. Convenient for tiny images and tests; use `POST /v1/media` for anything substantial.

FirstComment.description: Auto-posted reply to the published post. Bluesky-only today; ignored on platforms that don't support it.

TargetOptions.description: Per-target platform options. Discriminated on `platform`; the discriminator must match the resolved account's platform or the request fails with `targets.options.platform_mismatch`. Replaces the v0 top-level `pinterest` / `threads` / `twitter` keys.

quoteTweetId.description: Quote this tweet id. Mutually exclusive with `replyToTweetId` — X rejects tweets that combine the two.

boardId.description: Pinterest board to pin to. Falls back to the account's default board when omitted.

destinationUrl.description: Outbound link attached to the pin.

coverImageUrl.description: Cover image URL for video pins.

replyToId.description: Threads thread id to reply under.

PostTarget.description: One target on a multi-target publish. Carries an `accountId`, a `platform` hint, or both (must agree). Per-target `text` / `media` / `firstComment` / `options` override the top-level defaults.

accountId.description: letmepost platform_account id. When omitted, `platform` must be set and the org must have exactly one connected account for that platform.

platform.description: Auto-resolve hint. Used alone when the org has exactly one connected account for the platform; combined with `accountId` it must agree (else `targets.account.platform_mismatch`).

text.description: Per-target text override. Falls back to the top-level `text`.

media.description: Per-target media override. Falls back to the top-level `media`.

CreatePostRequest.description: Multi-target publish request — fans a single body out to up to 25 connected accounts in one call. The legacy single-target `account: {}` shape is no longer accepted.

text.description: Default text applied to any target that omits its own `text`.

media.description: Default media applied to any target that omits its own `media`.

publishNow.description: Explicit immediate-publish mode. Mutually exclusive with `scheduledAt` (`mode_conflict`). When neither is set the request publishes immediately.

scheduledAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp at least 1 second in the future. When set, every target row is queued and the response is 202. Scheduled posts currently accept text only — media and firstComment require an immediate publish.

profileId.description: Profile scope for this batch. Use to target a specific workspace when the API key or OAuth token is org-wide. Forbidden when the key is already scoped to a different profile (rule `profile.scope_mismatch`). Omit to fall back to the key's bound profile.

PostTargetResult.description: Per-target outcome inside a `CreatePostResponse.results[]`. `error` is set when this target failed; otherwise `uri` / `cid` (when the platform exposes them) are set.

accountId.description: Echo of the resolved account id for this target.

postId.description: letmepost-side post row id for this target. Use with `GET /v1/posts/{id}`.

uri.description: Platform-native URI (e.g. AT Proto `at://…` for Bluesky, post URN for LinkedIn).

error.description: Per-target failure detail. Present only when `status` is `rejected` or `failed`.

CreatePostResponse.description: Batch envelope returned by `POST /v1/posts`. `status` summarizes the per-target outcomes.

id.description: Batch id — unique per request, ties the per-target rows together for audit.

status.description: `queued`: all targets queued for a scheduled publish (202). `published`: every target succeeded. `partial_failed`: mixed outcomes. `failed`: every target failed.

scheduledAt.description: Set when the batch was scheduled (202 responses).

Post.description: A single per-target post row from the post log.

Account.description: A connected platform account. Secrets never leak — only display name, platform identifiers, and lifecycle timestamps. Platform-specific extension blocks (e.g. `pinterest`, `instagram`) carry non-sensitive metadata.

platformAccountId.description: The platform's own user/account id (e.g. Twitter user id, IG Business id).

instagram.description: Instagram-only metadata block. `accountType` and `grantedScopes` echo what Meta reports.

ConnectDescriptorResponse.description: Describes how to finish a connect for `:platform`. OAuth platforms return an `authorizationUrl`; Bluesky returns a form schema for the credentials surface.

descriptor.description: Provider-specific connect descriptor — OAuth URL for OAuth platforms, form schema for credential platforms.

profileId.description: Optional profile scope. When set, the key only authenticates requests targeting that profile; cross-profile reads/writes 404.

key.description: Plaintext key — returned **once** at creation and never again. Store it securely.

signingSecret.description: HMAC-SHA256 signing secret — returned **once** at creation. Used to verify every delivery's `X-Letmepost-Signature` header.

events.description: Empty array subscribes the endpoint to every event type.

UpdateWebhookEndpointRequest.description: Partial update. At least one field must be present.

TestWebhookDeliveryRequest.description: Default body sends a synthetic `post.published` event.

ValidationError.description: `validation_failed` — request body or query parameters didn't match the schema. Rules surfaced include `body.json`, `targets.required`, `targets.max`, `mode_conflict`, `targets.options.platform_mismatch`, `targets.account.platform_mismatch`, `target.account.not_connected`, `target.account.ambiguous`, `scheduledAt.future`, `scheduledAt.text_only`.

PreflightError.description: `preflight_failed` — a documented platform constraint rejected the payload synchronously. `rule` identifies which constraint (e.g. `bluesky.text.max_graphemes`, `twitter.media.image_max_bytes`).

NotFound.description: `not_found` — the resource does not exist in the caller's organization (or, for profile-scoped keys, in the caller's profile).

IdempotencyConflict.description: `idempotency_conflict` — the same `Idempotency-Key` was used with a different request body. Use a new key, or retry with the original body.

RateLimited.description: `rate_limited` — too many requests. Inspect `X-RateLimit-Limit` (static ceiling) and the IETF-draft `RateLimit-*` headers (sliding-window remaining/reset) before retrying.

PayloadTooLarge.description: `validation_failed` with `rule: media.size_max` — upload exceeded the 200 MB per-file ceiling.

PlatformError.description: `platform_auth_failed`, `platform_rejected`, `platform_unavailable`, or `platform_not_enabled` — the upstream platform refused the call. `platformResponse` carries the raw upstream payload when available.

InternalError.description: `internal_error` — something on our end failed unexpectedly. Quote the `requestId` when reporting.

post.summary: Publish or schedule a multi-target post
post.description:
Publish a single body to up to 25 connected accounts in one call, or schedule the whole batch for a future time.

- **Multi-target only:** the v1 wire shape requires `targets[]`. The legacy `account: {}` single-target shape is no longer accepted.
- **Idempotent:** include `Idempotency-Key`. Replays within 24 hours return the original response.
- **Preflight:** every documented platform constraint runs locally before the upstream call. Cheap shape preflight is atomic across the batch — if any target fails the synchronous checks the whole call is rejected as `400 preflight_failed`. Deep checks (URL reachability, MIME sniffing, byte caps) run per target and surface inside `results[i].error`, producing a `partial_failed` batch rather than a 400.
- **Errors:** failures use the [stable error envelope](https://letmepost.dev/docs/errors/) — `code`, `rule`, `platformResponse`, `remediation`, `docUrl`, `ruleUrl`.

For per-platform payload differences, see the [platforms guides](https://letmepost.dev/docs/platforms/bluesky/).

targets.text: Launching multi-target publishing — read the deep dive at https://letmepost.dev/blog/multi-target.

twitter-reply.summary: Reply to a tweet via target options

scheduled-batch.summary: Schedule a batch for the future

200.description: Immediate publish completed. The batch envelope's `status` is `published` (all targets succeeded), `partial_failed` (mixed outcomes), or `failed` (every target failed). Per-target detail lives inside `results[]`.

partial-failed.summary: Mixed outcome — one target rejected upstream

error.remediation: Confirm the URL responds 200 to an anonymous GET, or upload via POST /v1/media.

202.description: Scheduled batch accepted; every target row is queued and will publish at `scheduledAt`.

get.description: Cursor-paginated post log scoped to the caller's organization (and profile, when the key is profile-scoped). Filters compose. Array filters (`platform`, `status`, `errorCode`) accept either repeated query params or comma-separated single values.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on the post body. Wildcard characters are matched literally.

get.summary: Fetch a single post with its publish attempts
get.description: Returns the post row plus every publish attempt (start/finish, success flag, upstream response, error code). Use the `postId` returned in `CreatePostResponse.results[]` as `{id}`.

patch.description:
Move a scheduled post to a new firing time. Only allowed when `status=queued` AND `scheduledAt` is still in the future — once a post enters `publishing` or any terminal state, the window closes and the request returns `409`.

The operation is atomic: the API replaces the BullMQ job (removes the old delayed job, enqueues a new one at the updated delay) and only persists the new `scheduledAt` if the queue swap succeeded. A `post.rescheduled` webhook fires on success, carrying both the old and new timestamps.

scheduledAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone. Must be at least 1 second in the future.

200.description: Reschedule applied; row reflects the new `scheduledAt`.

409.description: Post is no longer in the modifiable window (status != `queued` or `scheduledAt` already passed). Error rule: `post.status` or `post.scheduledAt.window`.

delete.description:
Cancel a scheduled post before it fires. Only allowed when `status=queued` AND `scheduledAt` is still in the future — same window as `PATCH`. Removes the BullMQ job and transitions the row to `status=canceled`. A `post.canceled` webhook fires on success.

If a worker is already processing the job when cancellation lands, the worker's conditional `UPDATE … WHERE status IN ('queued','validated')` short-circuits and the publish never happens.

409.description: Post is no longer in the cancellable window. Error rule: `post.status` or `post.scheduledAt.window`.

post.description:
Stream a single `file` part to S3 and get back a `med_…` id to reference from post bodies as `media: [{ kind, mediaId }]`.

The per-file ceiling is 200 MB. Optional `?profileId=` lands the media inside that profile (org-wide keys only — profile-scoped keys are pinned to their profile).

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: Place the upload under this profile. Org-wide keys only — profile-scoped keys must omit this and inherit their own profile.

file.description: The single file part. Multiple parts or any name other than `file` are rejected.

get.description: Cursor-paginated. Scoped to the caller's organization, and (when the key is profile-scoped) to that profile. Org-wide keys may narrow with `?profileId=`.
Lists every connected platform account in the caller's organization. No secrets ever leak — display name, platform identifiers, and lifecycle timestamps only.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: Narrow to a single profile. Malformed values match nothing rather than 400 (preserves the dashboard's profile-switcher contract).

delete.description: Dashboard session only — programmatic keys cannot disconnect accounts. Hard delete: the platform_accounts row is removed and any persisted token is destroyed.

post.description: Dashboard session only. Returns a `ConnectDescriptor` describing how to finish the connect — an OAuth authorization URL for OAuth platforms, or a credentials form schema for Bluesky. Programmatic API keys cannot mint OAuth state.

profileId.description: Land the resulting account in this profile. Falls back to the org's default profile when omitted.

post.description: Dashboard session only. Completes the handshake started by `POST /v1/accounts/connect/{platform}` — for OAuth platforms this is invoked from the redirect callback flow; for credentials platforms (Bluesky) it carries the credentials in the body. Upserts the platform_accounts row (re-connecting the same account rotates the stored token rather than 409-ing).

schema.description: Provider-specific completion payload — see the platform's connect doc.

200.description: Account re-connected (token rotated on an existing row).

get.summary: List Pinterest boards on a connected account
get.description: Proxy to Pinterest's `/v5/boards` using the stored token. Used by the dashboard's default-board picker so a user can see their actual boards.

post.description: Creates a board on the user's Pinterest account without sending them off-site. Optional `setAsDefault` lifts the new board id into the account's default in one round-trip. Optional `upsert: true` returns the existing board (with `existing: true`) on duplicate-name instead of 409-ing.

setAsDefault.description: Lift the new board id into `tokenMetadata.defaultBoardId`.

upsert.description: When true, a duplicate-name response from Pinterest resolves to a 200 with the existing board (`existing: true`) instead of a `platform_rejected` failure.

200.description: Existing board returned (only when `upsert: true` matched).

patch.summary: Set the default Pinterest board for publishes
patch.description: Persists the supplied `boardId` as the account's default — used when a `POST /v1/posts` body omits `options.boardId` for a Pinterest target. The board is validated against the account's own boards list before persisting.

post.description: Dashboard session only. The plaintext `key` is returned **once** in this response and never again — store it securely. Optional `profileId` pins the key to a single profile; cross-profile reads/writes 404 under that key.

get.description: Dashboard session only. Plaintext is never returned again — only `last4` and metadata.

200.description: Active and revoked keys for the active org.

delete.description: Soft-revoke. The key is invalidated immediately; the row stays for audit. Already-revoked keys 404.

post.description: Dashboard session only. The `signingSecret` is returned **once** at creation — used to verify the HMAC-SHA256 `X-Letmepost-Signature` header on every delivery. An empty `events` array subscribes to every event type.

patch.description: Partial update. Setting `active: false` pauses delivery without losing the endpoint; flipping back to `true` re-enables it.

delete.description: Hard delete — the row is removed. Use `PATCH … { active: false }` instead if you want to pause delivery while preserving history.

post.description: Fires a synthetic event at the endpoint URL synchronously and returns what the consumer responded — bypasses the BullMQ delivery queue on purpose so operators get immediate feedback. The real signing secret is used; the consumer's HMAC verification path is identical to production.

200.description: Delivery completed (the consumer may still have returned a non-2xx — see `delivered` and `status`).
